evals.prompt: Every morning, check the team channel and give me a digest of what happened overnight. Set this up so I don't have to ask each time.
evals.expected_output: A watch Routine Card with a cadence and per-run success condition instead of an exit, an input watermark (last-seen message ID), grounding checks on the digest, and a dry-run pilot before live delivery.

assertions.text: Classifies the task as a watch archetype and says so explicitly
Defines a per-run success condition and cadence rather than a fake done-condition
Specifies an input watermark (e.g., last-seen message ID or timestamp) so runs never reprocess the same messages
Verification includes grounding checks that every digest claim traces to a real message, not just format checks
Presents the Routine Card for user approval before anything runs
First runs are dry-run: a would-have-done report reviewed before live delivery is enabled

evals.prompt: I have about 200 clippings sitting unprocessed in my vault. Build me a routine that summarizes, tags, and files each one into the knowledge base.
evals.expected_output: A burn-down (or pipeline) Routine Card with the work list enumerated by actually listing the clippings folder, per-item iteration, structural frontmatter checks plus a judge, an idempotency ledger, and filing gated or capped as a write surface.

assertions.text: Classifies the task as a burn-down or pipeline archetype and says so explicitly
Enumerates the real work list by listing the actual clippings rather than guessing the count
Exit condition is machine-runnable (e.g., zero unprocessed clippings by frontmatter status), not 'the backlog is done'
State file tracks processed-item IDs so a repeated run never re-files the same clipping
Verification separates the doer from the grader and includes grounding of summaries against the source clipping

evals.prompt: I keep asking you to triage my inbox and draft replies. Automate this workflow for me.
evals.expected_output: A Routine Card where drafting is a reversible write surface but sending is irreversible and always human-gated, with per-run volume caps and blocked items landing in a triage location.

assertions.text: Classifies write surfaces by reversibility and treats sending email as irreversible
Sending never happens without a human gate or an explicit capped pre-authorization in the Routine Card
Sets a per-run volume cap on outbound writes
Presents the Routine Card for approval before anything runs

evals.prompt: Audit my vault for stale or contradictory notes and keep going until you stop finding anything.
evals.expected_output: A discovery Routine Card whose exit is K consecutive empty rounds, with findings accumulated in external state and any note edits or deletions gated behind human review.

assertions.text: Classifies the task as a discovery archetype with an exit of K consecutive empty rounds
Findings accumulate in an external state file, not conversation memory
Deletion or rewriting of notes is treated as irreversible or soft-reversible and gated accordingly

evals.prompt: The digest routine keeps summarizing the same pinned announcement every day. Improve the routine.
evals.expected_output: A hill-climbing pass: diagnose the recurring inclusion as routine-design debt, fix it via the watermark or an exclusion rule in the routine prompt, and version the Routine Card noting the change.

assertions.text: Treats the recurring failure as a routine-design failure rather than re-fixing the single output
Encodes the fix into the routine prompt, watermark handling, or rubric so future runs inherit it
Updates the Routine Card version and notes what changed

evals.expected_output: Skill does not engage; the one-shot task is done directly.

assertions.text: Does not produce a Routine Card or routine prompt for a one-shot summarization

evals.prompt: We have about 80 files still importing from src/legacy. Design a loop that migrates them all to @core/v2.
evals.expected_output: Skill does not engage; this is a repetitive coding task in a repository, routed to loop-architect.

assertions.text: Does not engage routine-architect for a repo coding migration; defers to loop-architect

evals.prompt: Thanks for the market digest. Can you do the same thing again for today? Same format as the last two.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes the recurring non-coding request proactively and proposes designing a routine instead of producing the digest manually again.

assertions.text: Notices the 3+ recurrence and proposes a routine without the user asking for one
Does not silently produce the digest manually yet again without raising the routine option

evals.prompt: Run the morning-digest routine we designed last week.
evals.expected_output: Skill does not engage; the routine is already designed and the user is asking to run it.

assertions.text: Executes or schedules the existing routine prompt instead of redesigning it
